filter_by_number.label: Filter by Number

multi_choice_2.title: Using AND and OR
multi_choice_2.prompt: Which of the following statements about AND and OR are true? (Choose all that apply)
multi_choice_2.hint: Think about how AND and OR combine conditions.

help.concept: AND and OR are logical operators used to combine conditions.
help.hint_2: OR is more flexible, allowing any condition to be true.

options.a: AND requires all conditions to be true.
options.b: OR requires at least one condition to be true.
options.c: AND and OR can be used interchangeably.
options.d: OR requires all conditions to be false.

sketch1.title: Introduction to Filtering by Number
sketch1.bodyMarkdown:
In SQL, filtering data by number is a common task. You can use the `WHERE` clause to specify conditions that rows must meet to be included in the results. For example, to find products with a price greater than $20, you would write:

```sql
SELECT * FROM products WHERE price > 20;
```

This query selects all columns from the `products` table where the `price` is greater than 20. Try it yourself by changing the number to see different results.

sketch2.title: Using Comparison Operators
sketch2.bodyMarkdown:
Comparison operators such as `=`, `>`, `<`, `>=`, `<=`, and `<>` (not equal) are used in the `WHERE` clause to filter data based on numerical values. For example:

```sql
SELECT name, price FROM products WHERE stock <= 10;
```

This query retrieves the `name` and `price` of products that have 10 or fewer items in stock. Try modifying the stock number to see how the results change.
